hypothesis: If an outer SSL_read over HTTPS-origin-over-HTTPS-proxy returns WouldBlock after the inner proxy TLS stream has consumed or written bytes, a small bounded immediate retry could avoid a runtime wake and reduce nested TLS response-read latency.
implementation_tested: Wrapper progress marker for underlying async read/write byte movement.
AsyncSslStream::poll_read reset progress before each SSL_read and retried up to two times only after WouldBlock with observed underlying progress.
bench_tls_io counters and benchmark parser fields for ssl_read_pending_after_progress and ssl_read_progress_retries.

red_green_verification.green_result: passed after Wrapper progress marker implementation
passed after bounded retry policy implementation

retry_observation.interpretation: The hypothesized condition did not occur in the measured real nested TLS client path.

verdict: The candidate is a SOTA counterexample and must not be retained. The focused matrix worsened throughput, tail latency, CPU/request, and RSS, and the new counters show zero pending-after-progress events and zero progress retries.
follow_up: Remove progress-aware retry implementation and temporary parser/stat fields.
Keep this evidence and result directory as S9 causal narrowing evidence.
Continue S9 with a different root-cause hypothesis; this refutes OpenSSL/BIO progress-to-waker mismatch as the current nested TLS bottleneck.
